P.03
Colorado Design
Build Fly - v3
2025 - 2026
CU Boulder
Competition Day: Aircraft on the Flight LineThe completed v3 aircraft staged at the airfield ahead of competition flights. The twin-motor configuration reflects a clean redesign from v1, targeting improved thrust-to-weight ratio and STOL performance for the 2026 AIAA DBF banner-tow mission.
Wing Fabrication: Carbon Composite LayupComposite wing panel during build: black carbon fiber/fiberglass skin over a pink foam core, with a brushless motor already mounted at the tip. Patch repairs visible where the foam core was shaped and the skin was bonded.
Full Airframe: Workshop AssemblyWide view of the v3 airframe laid out across the workshop bench showing the full wingspan. The twin-motor wing tips and dark composite fuselage distinguish this design from the earlier foam prototype.
Avionics Integration: Wiring HarnessTwo team members routing the wiring harness through the fuselage, connecting ESCs, servos, and power distribution to the flight controller. The tight internal volume made component placement and cable management a real constraint.
Systems Check: Pre-Flight InspectionTeam member holding the assembled aircraft to assess balance and control surface alignment during a pre-flight systems check, confirming structural integrity before transport to the airfield.
Integration Review: Final AssemblyTeam inspecting the aircraft alongside the RC transmitter during final pre-flight checks, verifying control surface throws, motor direction, and power systems before heading to the flight line.
Bench Prep: Pre-Flight AdjustmentsPre-flight workbench with RC transmitter, wiring tools, fastener organizers, and the aircraft - the last round of adjustments before transport to the airfield for competition flights.
Nose Section: Motor & Propeller AssemblyClose-up of the nose motor mount and propeller assembly on the workbench. The brushless motor and spinner are fitted to the nose alongside the main landing gear - showing the structural integration of propulsion into the airframe.
Propulsion Engineer

Third-generation aircraft built by CU Boulder's AIAA Design/Build/Fly team for the 2025-2026 competition. The v3 is a full redesign from the ground up, moving from a foam prototype to a carbon composite airframe with a refined twin-motor propulsion system. The 2026 RFP required a Banner-Towing Bush Plane capable of STOL takeoff, banner deployment, and passenger payload missions.

  • Refined propulsion system selection for the v3 composite airframe, re-evaluating motor-propeller combinations against updated weight and drag estimates from the new wing geometry.
  • Supported avionics integration, routing power distribution and ESC wiring through the tighter internal volume of the composite fuselage.
  • Participated in pre-flight systems checks at the airfield, verifying motor direction, control surface throws, and CG balance before competition flights.
  • Contributed to composite wing fabrication, applying carbon fiber/fiberglass skins over foam core panels and mounting brushless motors at the wing tips.
